column1: Expanded Historical Timeline of Tartaria and Central Asian Peoples

column2: Event
Early Bronze Age cultures develop across the Eurasian steppe
Afanasievo culture in southern Siberia (possible Indo-European presence)
Sintashta culture emerges in the southern Urals (chariot warfare)
Andronovo culture spreads across Central Asian steppes
Early Indo-Iranian migrations through Central Asia
Karasuk culture develops in southern Siberia
Deer stone monuments appear across Mongolia and southern Siberia
Event
Scythian civilization flourishes across the Eurasian steppe, sometimes associated with territories later called "Tartary"
Cimmerians migrate across steppes and into the Near East
Saka culture (eastern Scythians) dominates Central Asia
Persian king Darius I invades Scythian territories in Central Asia
Xiongnu proto-state emerges in Mongolia
Yuezhi federation controls parts of Central Asia before being driven west by Xiongnu
Formation of the Xiongnu confederation under Modu Chanyu
Silk Road trade routes established through Central Asia
The Xiongnu defeat Chinese Han forces at the Battle of Mobei
Battle of Carrhae between Romans and Parthians (eastern power sometimes associated with "Tartary" in ancient accounts)
Event
Split of Xiongnu into Southern and Northern confederations
Xianbei people begin to replace Xiongnu dominance in Mongolia
Defeat of the Northern Xiongnu by the Xianbei
Sixteen Kingdoms period in northern China, many ruled by steppe peoples
Rouran Khaganate forms in Mongolia
Hunnic migrations begin to impact Europe
Height of power of the Rouran Khaganate
Hephthalite (White Hun) defeat of the Sassanid Persian Empire
Establishment of the Turkic Khaganate in Central Asia
Split of Turkic Khaganate into Eastern and Western halves
Tang dynasty conquers Eastern Turkic Khaganate
Arab conquest of Merv begins Islamic expansion into Central Asia
Second Turkic Khaganate established
Uyghur Khaganate established in Mongolia
Battle of Talas between Tang China and Abbasid Caliphate establishes Islamic influence in Central Asia
Fall of the Uyghur Khaganate
Establishment of the Khitan Liao Dynasty (often considered part of "Tartary" in medieval accounts)
Event
Fall of the Liao Dynasty to the Jurchen Jin Dynasty
Birth of Temüjin (later Genghis Khan)
Genghis Khan unites Mongol tribes, beginning the Mongol Empire (territories later referred to as "Great Tartary")
Mongol invasion of Jin Dynasty begins
Mongol conquest of the Khwarazmian Empire begins
Destruction of Samarkand by Mongol forces
Destruction of Merv by Mongol forces
Battle of the Kalka River - first Mongol encounter with Kievan Rus'
Death of Genghis Khan, empire divided among sons
Final defeat of Jin Dynasty by combined Mongol-Song forces
Kurultai approves invasion of Europe
Batu Khan's invasion of Kievan Rus' begins
Mongol sack of Kiev
Mongols reach Eastern Europe at the Battle of Liegnitz
Mongols withdraw from Hungary after death of Ögedei Khan
Seljuk Sultanate of Rum becomes Mongol vassal
Güyük Khan becomes Great Khan
Möngke Khan becomes Great Khan
Mongol conquest of Baghdad, end of Abbasid Caliphate
Death of Möngke Khan leads to fragmentation of the Mongol Empire
Battle of Ain Jalut - Mamluks defeat Mongols in Syria
Kublai Khan establishes capital at Khanbaliq (Beijing)
Kublai Khan founds the Yuan Dynasty in China
First Mongol invasion of Japan
Second Mongol invasion of Japan fails
Death of Kublai Khan
Conversion of Özbeg Khan of the Golden Horde to Islam
Fall of the Ilkhanate in Persia
Fall of the Yuan Dynasty and retreat of Mongols to the steppe
Event
Timur (Tamerlane) begins conquest of Central Asia
Timur establishes Timurid Empire with capital in Samarkand
Tokhtamysh of the Golden Horde sacks Moscow
Timur defeats Tokhtamysh at Battle of the Kondurcha River
Timur destroys Sarai, capital of the Golden Horde
Timur defeats Ottoman forces at the Battle of Ankara
Death of Timur, fragmentation of his empire
Establishment of the Oirat (Western Mongol) confederation
Ulugh Beg's astronomical observatory built in Samarkand
Esen Taishi unites the Oirat confederation
Esen Taishi captures the Chinese Emperor Zhengtong
Establishment of the Kazakh Khanate
Great Stand on the Ugra River - end of Mongol rule over Moscow
Establishment of the Khanate of Sibir
Final destruction of the Great Horde by Crimean Khanate
Babur (descendant of Timur) takes Samarkand
Babur establishes Mughal Empire in India
Russian conquest of Kazan Khanate begins expansion into territories called "Tartary"
Russian conquest of Astrakhan Khanate
Yermak's expedition begins Russian conquest of the Khanate of Sibir
Final defeat of the Khanate of Sibir by Russians
Event
First usage of "Great Tartary" on Western maps as a geographical designation
Treaty of Deulino acknowledges Russian control over Siberian territories
Publication of Purchas His Pilgrimes, popularizing term "Tartary" in English
Formation of the Dzungar (Jungar) Khanate by Oirat Mongols
Establishment of the Qing Dynasty by the Manchus (often considered part of "Chinese Tartary")
Construction of Russian Tobolsk fortress, administrative center of Siberia
Treaty of Nerchinsk between Russia and China establishes borders in parts of "Tartary"
Russian capture of Azov from Ottoman Empire
Beginning of Kamchatka expeditions by Vladimir Atlasov
Great Northern War begins, affecting Russian policies in Central Asia
Jungar (Dzungar) Khanate reaches its territorial height in Central Asia
First European map to show "Independent Tartary" as distinct region
"Barefoot Flight" of Kazakhs escaping Dzungar invasion
Treaty of Kiakhta defines Russo-Chinese border
Little Jüz of Kazakhs accepts Russian protection
Middle Jüz of Kazakhs accepts Russian protection
Beginning of Qing-Dzungar conflict
Qing conquest of Dzungaria (sometimes called "Little Tartary")
Dzungar genocide by Qing forces
Qing conquest of Altishahr (East Turkestan)
Kalmyk migration from Russia back to Dzungaria
Russian annexation of Crimean Khanate
Catherine the Great's "Greek Project" targeting Ottoman territories
Publication of Encyclopædia Britannica with extensive entry on "Tartary"
Event
Russian administrative reforms in Siberia affecting Tatar populations
Decembrist revolt affects Russian policies in frontier regions
Establishment of East Turkestan Khanate
Russians establish Fort Raim on the Syr Darya River
Treaty of Kulja between Russia and Qing China
Russian acquisition of Akmolin region (northern Kazakhstan)
Russian establishment of Fort Vernoe (Almaty)
Circassian genocide and expulsion in Caucasus
Russian conquest of Tashkent
Establishment of Russian Turkestan Governor-Generalship
Russian conquest of Bukhara
Russian conquest of Khiva
Russian annexation of the Khanate of Kokand
Yakub Beg's death ends East Turkestan independence
Treaty of Saint Petersburg finalizes Russo-Chinese border in Central Asia
Completion of Russian conquest of Turkestan (former "Independent Tartary")
Panjdeh Incident between Russia and Afghanistan
Trans-Caspian Railway reaches Samarkand
Beginning of Trans-Siberian Railway construction
New Russian settlements in the Hungry Steppe
Event
Revolution in Russia affects Central Asian territories
Central Asian Revolt against Russian conscription policies
Russian Revolution and civil war extend to former "Tartary" regions
Establishment of Alash Autonomy in Kazakh territories
Bukharan People's Soviet Republic established
Mongolian People's Republic established
Formation of USSR incorporates many former "Tartary" territories
National delimitation in Central Asia creates new Soviet republics
Collectivization begins in Central Asia
Kazakh famine begins, killing approximately 1.5 million
Great Purge severely impacts Central Asian intelligentsia
Deportation of Volga Germans to Central Asia
Deportation of Crimean Tatars to Central Asia
Virgin Lands Campaign begins in Kazakhstan
Rehabilitation of some deported peoples under Khrushchev
Jeltoqsan riots in Almaty against Russian appointment of Kolbin
Fall of the Berlin Wall begins collapse of Soviet system
Dissolution of the Soviet Union creates independent Central Asian states
Central Asian states join the United Nations
Shanghai Five (later Shanghai Cooperation Organization) established
Treaty on Deeper Military Integration between Russia, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, and Tajikistan
Event
Central Asian states provide support for NATO operations in Afghanistan
Tulip Revolution in Kyrgyzstan
Kazakhstan 2030 development strategy launched
Kyrgyz Revolution and ethnic violence in Osh
Establishment of Eurasian Economic Union
Kazakhstan joins World Trade Organization
Death of Islam Karimov, Uzbekistan's first president
Xinjiang internment camps established in former East Turkestan
Nursultan Nazarbayev resigns after 29 years as Kazakhstan's president
January protests and Russian troop deployment in Kazakhstan
Central Asian states maintain neutrality in Russia-Ukraine conflict
Expansion of China's Belt and Road Initiative in Central Asia
